title: USD Non-Variant Namespace Source Input Capability
purpose: Define the abstract input boundary consumed by variant composition. A variant namespace source needs a non-variant NamespaceSource with enough strength-ordered source-visible opinion data to compute variant selections and insert selected variant opinions at LIVERPS strength. This capability is intentionally not tied to payloads, references, or any specific composition arc provider.

included: no selected variant opinions already applied to the source-visible scene inputs
strength-ordered prim opinions for computing variant selections
source-visible variantSelection, variantSetNames, and variantSetChildren fields
capability declaration for which non-variant composition arcs are already represented
deferred: requiring any particular concrete provider such as payload, reference, or layer-stack namespace source
evaluating variants, recursive variants, fallback selections, or variants inside selected variants
asset resolution, file opening, and layer-stack construction

model.NonVariantNamespaceSourceInput: A NamespaceSource value that may include layer-stack, reference, payload, inherits, specializes, relocates, or future non-variant opinions, but has not yet applied selected variant opinions.
model.TerminalNonVariantProvider: The concrete graph node currently wired to satisfy this input. In this bounded graph it is usd-specializes-namespace-source, but the variant contract must not depend on that handle.
model.OpinionStack: A target-native strong-to-weak list of source-visible prim opinions sufficient for reading variantSelection and variantSetNames in strength order.
model.VariantSelectionPathLookup: The ability to inspect selected variant specs through paths validated by usd-paths without exposing selected variant paths to stage population.

laws: a variant namespace source consumes this abstract non-variant input rather than a payload-specific handle contract
the concrete graph may choose any terminal non-variant NamespaceSource provider that satisfies this capability
the input must expose source-visible prim opinions in strength order, or an equivalent target-native opinion stack, for the prims where variant selections are computed
strength-ordered opinion provenance must use typed composition provenance records and must not be stored as composite source:path strings
the input must preserve source-visible variantSelection, variantSetNames, and variantSetChildren fields with typed field-read semantics
the input must not have already applied selected variant opinions to the same exposed scene paths; variants are owned by the consuming variant provider
the input may include references and payloads, but neither references nor payloads are semantically required by this capability
when a bounded graph wires this input to a concrete provider such as usd-specializes-namespace-source, that wiring is an evaluation dependency and must not be read as LIVERPS strength order
stage population must consume the output variant NamespaceSource, not this non-variant input, when variants are in scope
adapter JSON may construct a concrete non-variant provider chain for tests, but domain APIs exchange NamespaceSource, path, token/name, and field value objects
